mapping.a_element: Schroedinger operator on a tiling whose spectral gaps are studied
mapping.b_element: Schroedinger operator on a graph whose spectrum is studied

mapping.a_element: discrete models on the tiling / its periodic identification
mapping.b_element: discrete Jacobi matrix obtained from the graph operator

brief_justification: Beyond the shared object 'Schroedinger operators', the machinery differs: paper A builds a groupoid C*-algebra of a tiling and uses its scaled ordered K_0-group (plus an invariant measure on the hull and the integrated density of states) as topological gap labels, whereas paper B applies an explicit operator duality reducing a continuous graph Schroedinger operator to a discrete Jacobi matrix (Kronig-Penney / Maryland-type reduction). K-theory of operator algebras versus a transfer/duality reduction are different mechanisms, and no role-for-role mapping between K_0-invariants and the Jacobi-matrix correspondence holds.
